sources: South Sudan National Revenue Authority: 3-Year Strategic Growth Plan
Financial Data Scout: CAGR-based Revenue Projection

2026.methodology: Projections for 2026 use a 3-year CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate) of 12% for non-oil revenue and an estimated 40% recovery in oil transit volumes. Spending is modeled based on the 2025/26 Fiscal Strategy Paper, prioritizing debt servicing and the clearance of salary arrears accumulated during the 2024-2025 fiscal shock.

sources: South Sudan Ministry of Finance and Planning: FY2024/25 Budget Update
World Bank Economic Monitor: South Sudan Oil Export Disruption Report
IMF Article IV Consultation - Supplementary Fiscal Data

2025.methodology: Calculation based on the 55% contraction in oil-sourced fiscal space due to the Sudan pipeline force majeure. Revenue figures account for the transition to aggressive non-oil tax enforcement. Spending reflects emergency prioritization of security and basic wage arrears over capital development projects.

sources: Ministry of Finance and Planning: Oil Revenue Projections
National Revenue Authority: Non-Oil Tax Collections

transfers.name: Transfers to States and Oil Agencies

2024.methodology: Data based on the FY 2023/24 Approved National Budget and the subsequent 2024/25 Budget Bill which revised the previous year's performance. Figures converted from South Sudanese Pounds (SSP) using official Ministry of Finance fiscal aggregates. Total spending includes the 'Oil for Roads' program which often bypasses standard treasury controls.
